title: G5: Role of the hypothesis n>=5 in the rank-one rigidity on the punctured grid

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: **Sub-lemma (threshold).** Identify and prove where $n\ge5$ is used. Candidate: for $n\ge5$ there are enough off-diagonal $2\times2$ boxes in each pair of modes so that the 'box graph' on $[n]$ (with the diagonal removed) is connected and every pair of indices co-occurs in a valid off-diagonal box, which is what lets local $2\times2$ minor vanishing propagate to global rank-one. Verify computationally that the rigidity fails (or degenerates) for $n=4$ but holds for $n\ge5$ (corresponds to P3a/G3).
